title: Focus on: Indigent study
description: GEORGE NEWS - Do you struggle to pay your...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Do you struggle to pay your municipal basic service charges? Help is available!

George Municipality cares and offers various means you can make use of to lighten your burden:


> Indigent subsidy


Council recognises that many residents cannot afford the full cost of services. The indigent subsidy is aimed at:

- Providing subsidy for basic service charges to the needy or qualifying indigent households.
- Ensuring affordability by subsidising tariffs.
- Providing procedures and guidelines for the allocation of the subsidy.
- Providing subsidy in a sustainable manner, within the financial and administrative capacity of Council.


> Application requirements for indigent subsidy


- You must reapply every year.
- The income from everyone in your household is assessed.
- The total household income may not exceed 2 x state pension (Sassa).
- Applicants must be full-time occupants.
- Applicants may not own any other property in SA.
- Applicants may not be a director of a company or in business.
- The subsidy only applies to property used for residential purposes.


> New electronic application process


- Only electronic applications will be accepted, to effectively record and keep track of the status of your application.
- No more hard copy applications to be filled in. Our officials will assist you with the electronic application.


> Subsidy of services categories (only residential properties)


- Water – 100% subsidy on the basic charge and 6kl per month, per household.
- Electricity – 100% subsidy up to 70kWh per month, per household.
- Refuse removal – 100% subsidy per month.
- Sewage disposal – 100% subsidy per month.


> Leakages, repairs and installation costs


The cost of the below can be recovered from the Equitable Share Grant.

- Water leakages
- Electricity meter problems
- Sewerage problems
- Conversion to pre-paid meter (PPM) as customers receiving the indigent subsidy must have a PPM
- Installation of smart and /or split meters


> Arrears on indigent accounts


After the indigent application is received and approved,

- All services related to arrears will be written off (once-off only),
- Indigent customers will be placed on restricted service levels (the PPM is blocked to limit escalation of debt)
- If the account is settled, restrictions will be lifted,
- Customers not fulfilling their responsibility with regard to the arrear debt:

• Will be placed on the pre-paid meter,• Up to 50% of electricity purchased will be allocated to the arrear debt.

Household visits:

Household visits are available to all elderly occupants or disabled occupants who require assistance at home with the online indigent application.

Where can I apply?

Main municipal building (cashier hall), an area office near you or at the debt pack office (Millwood Building, 83 Victoria Street, George)

Contacts:

044 801 9029 (George Municipality) 041 450 8676 (Debt Pack)

[email protected] [email protected]

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
